title: COPY/ADD --chmod rejects symbolic modes (only octal accepted) ‚Äî BuildKit parity gap
body:
## Summary

buildah's `COPY --chmod` / `ADD --chmod` accepts **octal numeric modes only**. It rejects all **symbolic** modes (`u+x`, `go-w`, `a+rX`, `a+rX,go-w`, ‚Ä¶) and the Go `0o755` prefix, all of which BuildKit accepts. This breaks building Dockerfiles that use BuildKit-style symbolic `--chmod` ‚Äî an increasingly common pattern.

## Empirical results

Minimal repro:
```
printf 'FROM scratch\nCOPY --chmod=<MODE> ctx.txt /ctx.txt\n' > Dockerfile && podman build .
```

buildah `1.44.0` (podman 6.0.0 server):

| `--chmod=` | result |
|---|---|
| `0755`, `0644` | ‚úÖ accepted |
| `0o755` | ‚ùå rejected |
| `a+rx`, `a+rwx` | ‚ùå rejected |
| `u+x` | ‚ùå rejected |
| `go-w` | ‚ùå rejected |
| `a+rX` | ‚ùå rejected |
| `a+rX,go-w`, `u+rX,go-w` | ‚ùå rejected |

Only plain octal (no `0o` prefix) is accepted. Every symbolic form ‚Äî including the simple `u+x` ‚Äî fails during `COPY`/`ADD` with `Error parsing chmod <mode>`.

## Impact

Dockerfiles using BuildKit-style symbolic `--chmod` cannot be built with buildah/podman. Real example: `COPY --link --chmod=a+rX,go-w . .`, which projects use to set "readable + traversable by non-root, no group/other write" in one layer instead of a separate `chmod -R` pass over a large tree.

The workaround is to rewrite the mode to octal, but that (a) must be reapplied per build, and (b) is not always a 1:1 conversion ‚Äî symbolic modes like `a+rX` are *conditional* on each file's existing mode (capital `X` = "execute only for dirs / already-executable files"), so a fixed octal constant is a lossy approximation.

## Request

Support symbolic `--chmod` modes in `COPY`/`ADD` for BuildKit parity, resolving each clause against the copied file's current mode (as BuildKit does). Even partial support for the common `a`/`u`/`g`/`o` ¬± `r`/`w`/`x`/`X` forms would unblock most real-world Dockerfiles.

## Environment

- buildah `1.44.0` (podman `6.0.0` server, macOS applehv machine)
- BuildKit (Docker buildx) accepts all the rejected forms

comments.body: AFAICT that was fixed recently in main https://github.com/podman-container-tools/buildah/commit/b0751fc6b83977e29c237eabf17849866edad1e2 and should be part of the next release 1.45
